question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted consensusEnd value of math.MaxInt64 to RetrieveBalanceAtBlock(), causing the latestBalanceBeforeConsensus query to scan all account_balance partitions with no upper bound, resulting in a full-table scan that delays block processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a consensusEnd timestamp that falls between two partition boundaries in selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound, causing partitionLowerBound to be set to 0 and forcing the latestBalanceBeforeConsensus query to scan the entire account_balance table, delaying block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a consensusEnd value that is far in the future relative to the current chain tip, causing the balanceChangeBetween query to perform a full scan of the crypto_transfer table over an unbounded timestamp range, effectively stalling block ingestion for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the deleted-entity branch (entity.Deleted != nil && *entity.Deleted) by querying a deleted account at a consensusEnd just after deletion, causing balanceSnapshotEndTimestamp = balanceChangeEndTimestamp - 1 to underflow to -1 when balanceChangeEndTimestamp is 0, producing an invalid timestamp range that causes the DB to scan backwards indefinitely and delay block processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp of 0 to getLatestBalanceSnapshot(), causing selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound to return no rows (partitionLowerBound stays 0), and then latestBalanceBeforeConsensus to return ab.ConsensusTimestamp == 0, triggering ErrNodeIsStarting which halts all block-dependent operations and simulates a block delay exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an alias value containing a specially crafted byte sequence to getCryptoEntity(), causing the selectCryptoEntityByAlias UNION ALL query (joining entity and entity_history) to perform expensive cross-table scans, consuming enough DB resources to delay block processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() with a consensusEnd value equal to the current latest block's consensusEnd, causing concurrent executions of getLatestBalanceSnapshot() and getBalanceChange() to contend on the same DB connection pool, starving block ingestion threads and causing a block delay exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp just before the genesis account_balance snapshot, causing selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound to return a lower_bound that is greater than the timestamp itself, making the latestBalanceBeforeConsensus query return no rows and triggering ErrNodeIsStarting, which blocks all subsequent block queries and simulates a 500%+ block delay?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a consensusStart equal to consensusEnd in getBalanceChange(), causing the balanceChangeBetween query (consensus_timestamp > @start AND consensus_timestamp <= @end) to return an empty range, and by flooding with such requests exhaust DB query slots, delaying block processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a consensusEnd of 1 (nanosecond epoch) to RetrieveBalanceAtBlock(), causing getLatestBalanceSnapshot() to find no partition lower bound and scan the entire account_balance table from timestamp 0, producing a query that runs for 500% or more of average block time and effectively freezes block-dependent API responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a consensusEnd that maps to a timestamp_range boundary in selectCryptoEntityByAlias (alias = @alias AND timestamp_range @> @consensus_end), causing the query to match both entity and entity_history rows and return ambiguous results, leading to incorrect block state that delays transaction finalization by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the nil entity path (entity == nil && accountId.HasAlias()) in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() by querying an alias that was never registered, causing the function to return a zero HbarAmount without consulting the block timestamp, and by doing so in bulk, cause the Rosetta API to serve stale block data that appears to freeze transactions for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getLatestBalanceSnapshot() to return ErrNodeIsStarting by supplying a consensusEnd timestamp that predates all account_balance rows for the treasury entity, making the mirror node appear to be starting and causing all block-dependent clients to stall for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-phase balance calculation in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() — first getLatestBalanceSnapshot() then getBalanceChange() — by timing requests to coincide with a block boundary, causing snapshotTimestamp to be from block N while balanceChangeEndTimestamp is from block N+1, producing a balance that includes transfers from a future block and corrupting the block state visible to other clients for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where entity.GetModifiedTimestamp() returns a value equal to consensusEnd, causing balanceChangeEndTimestamp = entity.GetModifiedTimestamp() and balanceSnapshotEndTimestamp = balanceChangeEndTimestamp - 1, and then supply a consensusEnd at a partition boundary so that balanceSnapshotEndTimestamp falls in a different partition, causing getLatestBalanceSnapshot() to scan an extra partition and delay block processing by 500% or more of average block time?
